reasoning: The article describes the synthesis and characterization of Co3O4/CuO hollow nanocage (HNC) nanocomposites, which exhibit enhanced oxidase-like activity. The nanocomposites were prepared by hydrothermal treatment of ZIF-67 in copper nitrate solution, followed by carbonization in air. The oxidase-like activity of the Co3O4/CuO HNCs was evaluated by catalytic oxidation of TMB in the absence of H2O2, and the reaction kinetics were analyzed. The nanocomposites were then used to develop a colorimetric biosensing method for dopamine (DA) based on its inhibiting effect on TMB oxidation. The method allows for DA detection with a limit of detection of 0.027 μM and a dynamic range of 0.05 - 8 μM. The article also includes detailed characterization of the nanocomposites using various analytical techniques.
